perturbation.original_text: 1.13 "Specifications" means the specifications for Bromelain SP set forth as Exhibit 1.13 hereto, as the same may be amended with the consent of both parties hereto, it being agreed that no amendment may be made thereto or refused which would *** Confidential treatment has been requested for redacted portions of this exhibit. This copy omits the information subject to the confidentiality request. Omissions are designated as [***]. A complete version of this exhibit has been provided separately to the Securities and Exchange Commission. 3 render Product incapable of application on humans or the use, supply or sale thereof in breach of any regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.13 "Specifications" means the general guidelines for Bromelain SP. These may be updated periodically.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Specifications' as those specifically set forth in Exhibit 1.13. The modified text broadens this definition to 'general guidelines' without reference to a specific exhibit, which effectively removes a critical defined component and replaces it with a vague one. This creates an in-text contradiction because later sections refer to the 'Specifications' as if they are clearly defined and agreed upon, which they are not in this modified scenario. Specifically Section 5 refers to Specifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without derogating from CBC's representations and warranties herein, CBC and MediWound shall work together in order to enable the CBC facility to accomplish all required standards, related to the manufacturing, packaging and delivering of Bromelain SP in accordance with the Specifications, GACP (Good Agricultural and Collection Practice) and cGMP (Current Good Manufacturing Practice) standards, ISO 22000 and all other applicable laws and regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Specifications' by direct reference to Exhibit 1.13, establishing a fixed, mutually agreed-upon standard. The changed text broadens the definition to 'general guidelines' that may be updated periodically, thus removing the specific exhibit reference. Section 5 references the 'Specifications' as the basis for manufacturing, packaging, and delivering Bromelain SP. The change to 'general guidelines' introduces ambiguity and undermines the enforceability of Section 5, creating a contradiction because it removes the fixed reference point for manufacturing standards.

perturbation.original_text: 6.4 Prior to delivery of each batch of Bromelain SP, CBC shall submit a batch sample to MediWound for inspection and approval. MediWound shall have the right, for a period of [***] days following receipt, to reject any Bromelain SP sample which: 6.4.1 fails to comply with MediWound's purchase order; or 6.4.2 fails to comply with the sample incoming inspection Specifications. Within the said [***] days, MediWound shall notify CBC of either: (i) its approval and acceptance of such batch sample ("Acceptance Sample Notice"); or (ii) its rejection of the batch sample in which case MediWound shall detail the reason(s) for the rejection of any such Bromelain SP sample. In the event of rejection by MediWound, CBC shall deliver complying Bromelain SP sample to MediWound within [***] days of rejection, free of cost (including transportation, duty, handling and insurance costs). For clarification purposes, MediWound's Acceptance Sample Notice in accordance with this section above shall in no event derogate from CBC's responsibilities hereunder. After CBC receives MediWound's Acceptance Sample Notice, CBC shall deliver the corresponding batch to MediWound for inspection and approval. MediWound shall have the right, for a period of [***] days following receipt, to reject any Bromelain SP batch which: 6.4.3 fails to comply with MediWound's purchase order; or 6.4.4 fails to comply with the batch incoming inspection Specifications. Within the said [***] days, MediWound shall notify CBC of either: (i) its approval and acceptance of such batch ("Acceptance Batch Notice"); or (ii) its rejection of the batch in which case MediWound shall detail the reason(s) for the rejection of any such Bromelain SP batch. In the event of rejection by MediWound, at CBC's request and expense, MediWound shall return any such Bromelain SP batch to CBC and CBC shall deliver complying Bromelain SP
perturbation.changed_text: 6.4 Prior to delivery of each batch of Bromelain SP, CBC shall submit a batch sample to MediWound for inspection. MediWound shall notify CBC of its inspection results, but acceptance or rejection is at MediWound's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 6.4 provides a defined process for sample approval and rejection based on specific criteria and timelines. The modified text removes the defined process, the right to reject the SP based on a specific time. Because MediWound still requires notification of inspection results, and because earlier in the document specifications are described, but the process of acceptance isn't, there's an in-text contradiction. In this specific scenario, specification are defined in section 1.13 but acceptance of the batch is not, it is mentioned to be at 'MediWound's sole discretion.' This creates uncertainty and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Invoices shall only be issued upon delivery of the Bromelain SP batch which shall take place only after CBC's quality control department has completed its testing and authorized delivery to MediWound, and MediWound's quality control department has provided CBC with an Acceptance Sample Notice for that batch and that the batch itself can be delivered.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a specific process for MediWound's acceptance or rejection of Bromelain SP batches, including timelines and criteria. The changed text removes this defined process, giving MediWound sole discretion without specific parameters. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that invoices are only issued after MediWound provides an 'Acceptance Sample Notice,' a term made ambiguous by the changed text. Because the changed text removes the objective process for Acceptance sample notice, the condition of the contradicted text cannot be met.

perturbation.original_text: 7A. Insurance In order to provide insurance coverage for CBC responsibilities, obligations and undertakings as set out under this Agreement and/or as required under any law with *** Confidential treatment has been requested for redacted portions of this exhibit. This copy omits the information subject to the confidentiality request. Omissions are designated as [***]. A complete version of this exhibit has been provided separately to the Securities and Exchange Commission. 13 respect to the manufacturing of Bromelain SP, CBC undertakes, at its sole cost and expense, to take out and maintain an "All risk" insurance against loss of and destruction or damage to the Facility (including fire, theft and vandalism, etc.), third party liability insurance, product liability insurance for the Bromelain SP and employers liability insurance. Without prejudice to the above, CBC shall maintain, or shall cause to be maintained with respect to itself and each of its Affiliates, such types and levels of insurance (including, without limitation, third party and product liability insurance), as are customary in the pharmaceutical or manufacturing industry to provide coverage for their activities contemplated hereby. Upon request of MediWound, CBC shall keep MediWound informed of the general parameters of its liability insurance program and any proposed substantive changes therein. Upon request, CBC shall furnish MediWound certification of insurance (and/or true copies of policies) showing the above coverage, signed by an authorized agent of the insurance company, certifying that liability assumed under this Agreement is fully insured without exception, and providing for at least thirty (30) days prior written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: 7A. Insurance CBC shall maintain general liability insurance. Upon request of MediWound, CBC shall keep MediWound informed of its liability insurance program.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 7A specifies detailed insurance requirements for CBC, including "All risk" insurance, third-party liability, product liability, and employer's liability insurance. The modified text drastically reduces these requirements to simply 'general liability insurance'. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 7.1, where CBC is responsible for defending MediWound from patent infringement claims, as general liability might not cover intellectual property disputes. The omission of the specific insurance types leaves ambiguity regarding whether patent infringement would be insured against.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CBC shall defend and assume responsibility for any suit, claim or other action by a third party alleging that MediWound's use of Bromelain SP infringes any patents or other rights of such third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies detailed insurance requirements for CBC, including 'All risk' insurance, third-party liability, product liability, and employer's liability insurance. The modified text drastically reduces these requirements to simply 'general liability insurance'. This creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, where CBC is responsible for defending MediWound from patent infringement claims, as general liability might not cover intellectual property disputes. The omission of the specific insurance types leaves ambiguity regarding whether patent infringement would be insured against, which is a significant contradiction.
